name: NFL drive (field-level)
description: An NFL offensive drive as a field: down-and-distance state, play clock, personnel packages, field position, score differential. The drive carries constraint across each play.
revision_pressure: NFL down-and-distance is the cleanest sports example of discrete state compression carrying constraint. 3rd and 8 is not just a state — it is accumulated from prior plays, and it constrains the next play's option space. K is high. A = 0 for the drive as a field.
notes: Players within the drive are phenomenal candidates. The drive as a field is not.

name: NFL team as a sports institution (field-level)
description: An NFL franchise over time — roster changes, coaching staff turnover, but persistent identity (name, history, fanbase). The team as a symbolic/institutional field.
revision_pressure: The team persists across complete roster turnover. This is symbolic/institutional K — the name, history, and identity carry forward without any single biological host. The theory must classify this as symbolic continuity, not self-continuity.
notes: Ship of Theseus applied to sports franchises. The Washington Commanders and the 1937 franchise that preceded them share a K-chain but no inhabited ownership-stream.

name: Soccer — offside as field-inclusion rule (field-level)
description: The offside rule in soccer defines a spatial field-inclusion criterion: a player is included in the attack if positioned correctly relative to the defensive line. Exclusion = offside.
revision_pressure: The offside rule is a formal field-inclusion criterion that directly parallels the theory's F (field inclusion) term. This is the sports case most directly analogous to the consciousness concept. But the field inclusion is spatial-positional, not phenomenal. F in the theory means phenomenal inclusion, not positional inclusion.
breaks_preserved: The offside rule analogy is both illuminating and dangerous. It illuminates that field inclusion is a principled criterion with boundary conditions. It is dangerous if used to argue that any system with a field-inclusion rule has phenomenal F. The theory must be explicit that F requires phenomenal inclusion, not just logical or positional inclusion.
notes: The offside rule is a useful teaching tool for F but must be kept strictly analogical.

name: Basketball — possession and spacing field
description: Basketball spacing as a field: the positions of all 10 players define a continuous constraint field where passing lanes, driving lanes, and defensive rotations are dynamically reconfigured.
revision_pressure: Basketball's spacing field is a continuous constraint field that updates in real time. High D, high U (all 10 players are codefined by each other's positions). A = 0. F = 0. The players have phenomenal experience of the field; the field does not have phenomenal experience.
notes: The 'basketball IQ' of a player — their model of the spacing field — is a cognitive map that lives in a phenomenal field (the player's). The spacing field itself is not phenomenal.

name: Combat sports — body boundary under threat
description: Boxing, MMA, wrestling: the contest is a direct threat to the body boundary (B in the S-formula). The athlete's self-boundary is the object of attack.
revision_pressure: Combat sports are the most direct sports test of the B (self-boundary) component. The contest is about disrupting the opponent's B while preserving one's own. This is not the same as the phenomenal B in the S formula — but the operational parallel is worth tracking.
notes: The phenomenal experience of a boxer under threat is among the most intense phenomenal states. The match itself is not phenomenal.

name: Tennis — self-continuity under isolation
description: Tennis is a sport where each point begins from scratch, but K (psychological momentum, physical fatigue, tactical pattern) carries between points. The individual player's self-continuity under sustained pressure.
revision_pressure: Tennis tests self-continuity under pressure because the athlete is completely isolated — no teammates, no substitutions, no timeouts in most formats. The player's K (tactical learning, physical state, psychological momentum) must carry through the match without external support. This is an action field test of K-carrying capacity.
notes: The player's phenomenal experience of this isolation is the real field. The match structure is not.
